chunk_summary: Clinic-to-community coordination in India emphasizes aligning clinic advice with community systems like ICDS and POSHAN, using shared tools and follow-up to improve complementary feeding for children aged 6–24 months.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of clinic-to-community coordination in improving complementary feeding practices in India.
Learn how to document and communicate feeding goals and nutrition data for ASHA and anganwadi workers.
Identify key prophylaxis schedules and referral protocols for malnourished children.
Recognize the importance of follow-up and practical counselling for high-risk children.
key_facts: Complementary feeding counselling should align with ICDS/anganwadi services and POSHAN platforms.
Document four domains: texture stage, meal frequency, missing food groups, and food safety corrections.
Prophylaxis includes IFA syrup, vitamin A, and deworming as per national schedules.
Referral notes for wasting should include MUAC or weight-for-length/height triggers and complications.
High-risk children require defined follow-up dates and practical counselling with feeding tools.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Complementary feeding counselling succeeds when messages are repeated across platforms. In India, clinic advice should align with community systems such as ICDS/anganwadi services, the POSHAN platform, and routine home visits by ASHA and anganwadi workers (AWW).
document_key_topics: Prophylaxis and follow-up for high-risk children
chunk_heading: COMPLEMENTARY FEEDING 6–24 MONTHS;MALNUTRITION TRIAGE AND SPECIAL POPULATIONS;CLINIC-TO-COMMUNITY COORDINATION IN INDIA;
